title: SCHC link-local scope check: Python fe80::/64 vs Rust/C fe80::/10 (+ Rust/C round-trip self-corruption)
description: Python headers.py:57-58 _is_link_local requires top 64 bits == fe80::/64. Rust codec.rs:150-152 / headers.rs:123-125 and C schc.c:159-162 check fe80::/10. For addresses inside fe80::/10 but outside fe80::/64 (reserved per RFC 4291): Python -> rule 255; Rust/C -> rule 0/2/3/4 and then CORRUPT THEMSELVES on round-trip since decompress hardcodes the fe80:: prefix (Rust codec.rs:29, C schc.c:963-965). Rust and C are in parity with each other (identical wrong behavior); Python is self-consistent. Low practical severity (such addresses are invalid) but input-dependent behavioral divergence. Fix: converge on fe80::/10 with a decompress-side guard, or fe80::/64 everywhere.
